name: RULE FLIP
conditioned_path: FYP format-flipping — the feed that re-novelties itself by swapping layouts (text → photo → video → carousel) before you habituate
mechanic: Tap the green tile, dodge the red — except every 4 seconds the rule inverts: now tap red, dodge green, then tap nothing, then tap both. The brain that craves novelty gets it from a rule that keeps flipping, not an endless feed. Survive 10 flips and it ENDS.
video_trick: rule-inversion bait — 'wait, it just flipped again' keeps the hook clip re-watching

name: MICRO MASH
conditioned_path: TikTok trend-mashup feeds — rapid-cut comps of 15 different trends stitched into one 30s clip
mechanic: A fixed deck of 15 micro-games, each 3 seconds with a one-word instruction (TAP, HOLD, SWIPE, DODGE, LIFT, CATCH). Clear one, the next flashes up instantly — a fresh novelty hit every 3s. The deck has a bottom card: clear all 15 and it prints FIN. Variety shuffle with a real ending.
video_trick: WarioWare-style rapid-cut — each 3s clip is a different mini-hit, the pace is the hook

name: VERB BLINK
conditioned_path: the one-tap verb UI of Stories/Reels — tap, hold, swipe-up mapped to a dozen actions
mechanic: A single verb flashes center-screen for 2 seconds then swaps: TAP, HOLD, SWIPE UP, DON'T MOVE, DOUBLE-TAP. Execute the verb on the target before it changes; one wrong move ends it. The novelty is the rule churning faster than you can settle into it — then it stops.
video_trick: anticipation agony — 'what's the next word gonna be' keeps eyes locked

name: GENRE SHUFFLE
conditioned_path: the FYP genre-whiplash — cooking clip, then prank, then dance, then ASMR, back to back
mechanic: Every 10 seconds the game swaps genre entirely: catch falling dots → dodge rising bars → tap the lit tile → trace the line. One thumb, four genres in 40s, then FIN. The format-flip IS the novelty — the same way the FYP hits you with a different genre each swipe, except here it ends.
video_trick: genre-whiplash — 'wait this is a totally different game now' is the rewatch trigger

name: COLOR FLIP
conditioned_path: thumbnail churn — trending color palettes that re-novlety the whole feed at once
mechanic: The whole screen cycles through colors; a target color flashes for 1 second — tap the instant the background matches it. The target color changes each round and the cycle speeds up. 12 quick-win bursts, then a clean stop. The novelty hit is a fresh target every beat.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — the almost-match you mistimed haunts the rewatch

name: SWITCH STANCE
conditioned_path: control-scheme shifts mid-scroll — swipe gestures that invert between apps without warning
mechanic: One endless runner track; every 6 seconds the control scheme flips: left/right invert, tap-to-jump becomes tap-to-duck, swipe-up becomes swipe-down. Adapt to the new stance or crash. The novelty is your own muscle memory betraying you — and a crash ends it.
video_trick: shock-honesty — 'I just died because the controls flipped' is the share line

name: ODD RULE
conditioned_path: spot-the-difference / 'which one doesn't belong' trend clips
mechanic: Spot the odd tile in a grid — but the definition of 'odd' changes each round: odd color, then odd shape, then odd count, then odd rotation. You re-discover the rule every 5s, exactly the pattern-hunt the FYP feeds. 15 rounds, then done.
video_trick: play-along comment bait — 'can you spot it before round 8?'

name: TEMPO FLIP
conditioned_path: trending-audio swap — the same dance clip re-cut to a faster/slower sound every post
mechanic: Tap a pulsing dot on the beat; every 8 beats the required tempo flips — double-time, then half-time, then off-beat-only. Quick-win bursts of rhythm adaptation. The novelty is the tempo itself churning. Six tempos and it resolves.
video_trick: participation-comment-bait — 'I cracked at tempo 5'

name: MASH DECK
conditioned_path: the shuffled Discover feed — no two cards in a row are the same topic
mechanic: A shuffled deck of 20 micro-tasks, each a different 3s verb-action (tap 3, hold 1s, swipe left, avoid red, catch 2). Tap to draw the next card; clear the whole deck to win. Each card is a fresh mini-hit and the deck has a bottom — the novelty hit, then a stop.
video_trick: variable-reward roulette — you never know which card is next

name: WIN FLIP
conditioned_path: algorithm re-ranking — the thing that won yesterday flops today, the rules silently invert
mechanic: A simple tap game, but the win condition inverts every 5 seconds: tap FAST to score, then tap SLOW to score, then DON'T tap to score, then tap only on green. The brain that craves novelty gets it from rule-flips instead of new content — and a wrong move ends it.
video_trick: shock-honesty — 'the win condition literally flipped on me'

name: QUICK QUEST
conditioned_path: the 5-part 'wait for it' challenge reel — a stacked to-do list of micro-dares
mechanic: A fixed quest log of 10 micro-missions, each 5 seconds and totally different: catch 3, avoid 2, hold 1s, swipe 4, trace the line. Check one off, the next appears. The quest log visibly completes — every open loop actually closes, then FIN.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — watch the quest log tick down to zero

name: INPUT ROULETTE
conditioned_path: app onboarding — every new screen demands a different gesture you've never seen
mechanic: Each round flashes a random input icon (tap, long-press, swipe, double-tap, drag) for 2 seconds; do the right gesture before the window closes. The demanded verb is the novelty — re-learn the move each round. Streak builds until a wrong gesture ends it.
video_trick: split-second judgment — 'tap or hold?!' panic is the clip

name: MINI BOSS RUSH
conditioned_path: viral 'final boss' trend chains — each clip escalates to a harder version of the last
mechanic: Eight mini-bosses, each a distinct 10s micro-challenge: dodge a spiral, tap a shrinking zone, hold through static, steer a falling shard. Beat one and the next slams in. The novelty is a brand-new boss every 10s — and beating the 8th prints FIN.
video_trick: competitive rank — 'I got to boss 7, beat that'

name: SORT SHIFT
conditioned_path: re-categorization churn — the algorithm silently re-tags your interests mid-session
mechanic: Items fall into two lanes; swipe each to the right bin — but the sorting rule changes every 4 seconds: by color, then by shape, then by size, then by symbol. Quick re-categorization every few beats; one mis-sort ends it. The rule churns like a feed re-tagging itself.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — the mis-sort at the rule-flip haunts you

name: SPIN RULE
conditioned_path: trend-roulette — the wheel-of-trends format where each spin lands on a fresh challenge
mechanic: A spinner lands on a new rule every 4 seconds — TAP ALL, TAP NONE, TAP ONLY BLUE, TAP THE BIGGEST, AVOID RED. Execute the landed rule on the tiles before it spins again. The wheel is the novelty engine; 12 spins and the session closes.
video_trick: variable-reward roulette — the spin itself is the cliffhanger

name: RULE STACK
conditioned_path: feature-creep feeds — each scroll adds a new UI rule on top of the last
mechanic: Rules compound each round: round 1 'tap red', round 2 'tap red but not if it's big', round 3 'tap red small but only on the left'. Every round adds one rule to the stack — how deep can you hold the whole rule-set before you slip? The novelty is a thicker ruleset each beat.
video_trick: participation-comment-bait — 'I held 6 rules, you?'

name: FLICK FLIP
conditioned_path: trending-challenge flips — the same challenge re-posts with the rules subtly inverted
mechanic: Flick tiles into the matching bin; every 5 seconds the bins silently swap labels — left was recycle, now left is trash. Quick adaptation to a rule that flips like a re-posted trend. One wrong bin ends it; the format-flip is the whole game.
video_trick: shock-honesty — 'the bins just swapped on me' is the clip

name: PATTERN BREAK
conditioned_path: the 'what changed?' edit-bait clip — same clip, one frame altered, spot it
mechanic: A pattern flashes, then repeats with one element changed — tap the changed element. But what counts as 'the pattern' rotates each round: color change, then position change, then size change. Re-orient your attention every 5s, exactly the orienting response the FYP farms. Ends at a miss.
video_trick: play-along comment bait — 'did you catch round 9?'

name: NOVELTY GARDEN
conditioned_path: the novelty-craving itself — the brain that wants a fresh hit every few seconds, met without the spike
mechanic: A daily deck of 10 tiny varied sensory micro-toys — pop one, ripple one, spin one, tap one — each a fresh little input, but slow and unhurried. Draw one card at your own pace; the deck has a last card, and finishing plants a small garden tile. Bounded variety with no spike, then it's done for the day.
video_trick: the feed that ends — novelty that closes itself instead of scrolling forever

name: SHUFFLE SETTLE
conditioned_path: the shuffled-feed craving — the want for 'something different' untied from any urgency
mechanic: A shuffled deck of gentle one-tap micro-prompts — tap a dot, hold a breath, swipe a petal, lift a leaf — each card a different tiny verb drawn at breath pace. The deck has a bottom card; the screen softly dims as it empties. Variety as a calm walk that genuinely ends.
video_trick: calm completion — the variety itch scratched, then the lights go down
